results.name: array_sum_bounded
results.description: Sum elements of a bounded array. C: no bounds check, UB on out-of-bounds. Rust: slice with bounds checking.

results.name: struct_field_access

counterexample.reason: null_pointer_dereference: C UB, Rust disallows null references

results.description: Access fields of a point struct. C: direct memory offset. Rust: safe field access.

results.name: buffer_copy

counterexample.reason: buffer_overflow: C UB (writes past end), Rust panics (length mismatch)

results.description: Copy n bytes between buffers. C: memcpy with no overlap check. Rust: copy_from_slice with length check.

results.name: linked_node_value

counterexample.reason: null_dereference: C UB, Rust Option::unwrap panics

results.description: Access value from a node pointer. C: direct dereference, UB if null. Rust: Option unwrap, panic if None.

results.name: array_index_negative

counterexample.reason: negative_index: C UB (ptr arith underflow), Rust usize wraps to huge value -> panic

results.description: Array access with potentially negative index. C: UB. Rust: panic (usize wraps).

results.name: pointer_arithmetic_offset

counterexample.reason: ptr_past_allocation: C UB, Rust checked pointer arithmetic

results.description: Compute p + offset. C: UB if result outside allocation. Rust: checked pointer arithmetic.

results.name: string_length_scan

counterexample.reason: unterminated_string: C strlen reads past allocation (UB), Rust &str has known length

results.description: Scan for null terminator. C: reads until \0, UB if unterminated. Rust: slice len() is O(1).

results.name: struct_padding_layout

counterexample.reason: struct_layout_divergence: C padding vs Rust repr(Rust) layout differs

results.description: Struct with different padding in C vs Rust. C: implementation-defined padding. Rust: repr(C) matches, default repr may differ.
